text:
There once was a lonely boy named Theodore. He loved rabbits so much that he could hardly bear to look away. One day he discovered a tiny orphaned squirrel. Ted could hear little things squirrels sometimes did. They would chirp softly when alarmed.

For weeks ted would watch the little squirrel scamper through dappled sunlight. It was shy. Ted fed it nuts. One day he stayed home. They stayed awake together. They snoozed. Ted saved a nut for tomorrow.

Never came tomorrow.

The sun went down. Ted searched everywhere. Finally he found the little squirrel curled tight. Ted couldn. t wake it. It was cold.

When dawn broke Ted saw rescue wings.

A hawk sat on a tree branch. Nothing would persuade it to drop its prize. Until Ted whispered.

"Please."

Slowly?

"Yes."

The hawk threw feathers. Ted scooped them up. They went home. Ted nursed the squirrel back to health. And somewhere deep down the owl knew
